predecessor: baseline-round3-2026-07-20.json (round 3, against e8220e0, before the BÍN-inflection fixes, the 'er með' cue, and the methodology-field extractor)
method: Between round 3 and this round, the user redirected two open items with explicit primary-source research requirements: (1) Wikipedia research on Sósíalistaflokkurinn/Vinstrið/VG before any 'Vinstrið' aliasing decision (already resolved pre-round-3, produced party-ontology-2026.json), and (2) a hard requirement to verify every party regex against málið.is/BÍN case-inflection tables rather than trust \w* by inspection, producing reference/party-inflections-bin.json and 3 real regex fixes. This round closed the two remaining round-3 open items ('er með' cue, methodology extractor), verified the BÍN fixes hold under regression, and built the methodology-field extractor end-to-end including two internal regex bugs found and fixed while building it.
headline_findings: Both of round 3's carried-over open items are now closed: the 'er með'/'eru með' poll-cue construction (found independently in two real articles across rounds 2-3 before being added, per this skill's evidence-before-cue rule) and the methodology-field extractor (sample_size, response_rate_pct, fielded_note), wired into both the RÚV and Vísir fetch paths for every article regardless of whether its topline numbers came from a chart or from prose.

The 'er með' fix is verified to change real output, not just pass a unit test: visir-20262884571 went from 5 to 6 extracted parties (Sjálfstæðisflokkur 31.3% newly captured), with the tense-symmetric 'var/voru með' added to the historical-cue side at the same time so a past-tense 'var með' figure isn't mistaken for the current poll result.

extract_methodology() needed two real internal regex fixes before it worked correctly on real dates — a naive '[^.]+\.' pattern truncated at the period inside an ordinal date ('5.'), and the word-count-bounded fix that followed it then over-captured into the next sentence on a short date. The final version stops at the first 4-digit year instead of counting tokens, verified against two differently-phrased real articles and one live end-to-end fetch (visir-20262904348: sample_size=12102, response_rate_pct=38.5, fielded_note='1.–30. júní 2026').

Unplanned finding while answering Q12: methodology extraction genuinely runs on chart-sourced articles too, not just prose-fallback ones — ruv-468092 (chart-sourced, 10 parties via Highcharts) independently yielded response_rate_pct=50.0 from its own prose despite its topline numbers coming from the chart. This confirms the deliberate 'paragraphs and extract_methodology() run unconditionally, not gated behind the prose-fallback branch' design decision was correct and is doing real work, not dead code.

The Heimildin-phrasing gap for sample_size, documented as fully unmatched in this round's initial write-up, turned out to be more nuanced on direct testing: response_rate_pct actually DOES resolve for Heimildin's construction (_RESPONSE_RATE_RE matches on the shared 'svarhlutfall' root regardless of surrounding phrasing) — only sample_size stays unresolved. SKILL.md was corrected to state this precisely rather than the coarser 'both fields unmatched' claim originally drafted.

results.answer: Yes, unchanged after the BÍN fixes: ruv-451831=8 parties, ruv-428434=7, ruv-467932=9, ruv-468092=10 — identical counts to round 3's baseline for the same four articles.

Both — each fix was first proven against a hand-written regex test (e.g. _PARTY_RE.search() on the literal BÍN-listed case form), then the four regression articles above were re-fetched live and produced identical output to before the fixes, confirming no real article's extraction changed (expected: none of the four regression articles happen to use the specific inflected forms the fixes targeted — the fixes protect against future articles using 'Vinstrinu'/'Vinstrisins'/'Pírötum'/non-nominative full-legal-name mentions, none of which appear in this particular fixed regression set).
Yes — added to _POLL_CUE_RE as `(?:er|eru)\s+með`, verified directly: `_POLL_CUE_RE.search('Sjálfstæðisflokkur er með 31,3 prósenta fylgi')` matches.

visir-20262884571 (round 2/3, 'Sjálfstæðisflokkur er með 31,3 prósenta fylgi') and a second independent real occurrence found in round 3's data before this round's fix was applied — both required before adding, per this skill's own stated rule of never adding a cue on a single example.
Yes — `(?:var|voru)\s+með` added to _HISTORICAL_CUE_RE in the same change, tense-symmetric with the existing mælist/fékk present/past distinction.
One — visir-20262884571 went from 5 to 6 rows (Sjálfstæðisflokkur 31.3% newly captured), verified via a real fetch re-run this round, matching the count already recorded in round 3's Q15 gap note.
Yes — `_SAMPLE_SIZE_RE` matches `heildarúrtak`/`í úrtaki voru` phrasing, verified: 12102 and 3406 extracted correctly from two differently-worded real sentences.

Yes — `_RESPONSE_RATE_RE` matches `þátttökuhlutfall`/`svarhlutfall` phrasing with filler-word tolerance, verified: 38.5 and 44.7 extracted correctly, plus a third real construction (Heimildin's, Q16/Q17) also resolved to 50.0 despite not being the pattern originally targeted.

Yes — `fielded_note` holds the raw field-date phrase as free text (e.g. '1.–30. júní 2026', '5. til 31. janúar 2026'), deliberately not parsed into structured start/end dates since Icelandic date-range separators aren't consistent enough to be worth parsing when the raw phrase is already human-readable.

Two differently-worded real examples, not one: 'Heildarúrtak var X og þátttökuhlutfall Y prósent' and 'Í úrtaki voru X ... en þátttökuhlutfall var Y prósent' — different sentence structure, different filler words, both resolve correctly.

Yes — `fetch visir-20262904348` (a live, non-cached fetch, raw JSON and CSV both deleted first) produced sample_size=12102, response_rate_pct=38.5, fielded_note='1.–30. júní 2026' end-to-end, not just via the isolated extract_methodology() unit call.

Both — confirmed directly this round: ruv-468092 is chart-sourced (10 parties via Highcharts, source='chart') and still independently yielded response_rate_pct=50.0 from its own prose. Paragraph extraction and extract_methodology() were deliberately moved outside the 'if not parties' prose-fallback gate specifically so this would work, and this is the first round that actually checked it did.

Bug 1: the original `[^.]+\.` pattern assumed a period always ends a sentence, but Icelandic ordinal dates ('5. til 31. janúar 2026') contain periods after each ordinal number, so it truncated the capture at '5.' — the first period encountered, not the sentence end. Bug 2: the fix that followed (a bounded word-count capture, `(?:\S+\s+){1,8}\S+`) then over-captured on a *short* date: '1.–30. júní 2026.' is only 3 tokens, so an 8-token window let the match continue past the real sentence boundary into the next sentence's 'Heildarúrtak var 12.102 og þátttökuhlutfall 38,5'. Both found via direct testing against the two real phrasings, not hypothesized.

Because the pattern's stop-condition (any '.') fires on ordinal-number periods, which are grammatically required after every Icelandic ordinal ('5.', '31.') and have nothing to do with sentence boundaries — the regex had no way to distinguish an ordinal's period from an actual full stop.

Because a fixed token count can't adapt to variable-length dates — a window wide enough to capture a long date ('5. til 31. janúar 2026', 5 tokens) is also wide enough to run past a short date ('1.–30. júní 2026.', 3 tokens) into whatever text follows it in the same paragraph-joined string. The final fix replaces the fixed count with a non-greedy match that stops at the first 4-digit year token instead, which scales to the actual date length rather than a guessed constant.

Partially — response_rate_pct resolves to 50.0 for Heimildin's construction ('...er svarhlutfallið því rétt rúmlega 50 prósent') since _RESPONSE_RATE_RE matches on the 'svarhlutfall' root regardless of surrounding phrasing. sample_size stays unresolved (None) for this construction.
results.gap: This is a more precise answer than SKILL.md's first draft claimed ('both fields unmatched') — corrected during this round after directly testing the Heimildin sentence rather than assuming from the sample_size gap alone.

results.answer: Both of round 3's listed next steps were resolved: the methodology-field extractor (round 3's #1 priority) and the 'er með' cue (round 3's #2). Round 3's #3 (a systematic chart-vs-prose completeness check) was not attempted this round — still open.

The systematic chart-vs-prose completeness check (first flagged round 3, still untouched) is now the longest-standing single open item — confirming chart extraction isn't silently missing a party the article's own prose also discusses, on a sample large enough to trust, rather than the one-off spot-checks done so far. VB discovery (never started) and Heimildin fetch (discovery only) are larger in scope but have been consistently deprioritized in favor of correctness fixes on the already-built RÚV/Vísir paths across all four rounds — a deliberate choice, not an oversight, since a wrong number on a working source is worse than a missing source.

gap_patterns_round4: This round's two headline fixes ('er með', methodology extractor) were both carried directly from round 3's highest_value_next_steps list rather than discovered fresh — a sign the eval loop is converging: new rounds increasingly close previously-identified gaps rather than surfacing entirely new ones, in contrast to round 3 where scale-testing (a real --all batch) surfaced two previously-unknown bugs neither round 1 nor round 2 had found.

The Heimildin-phrasing check (Q16) is a good example of why 'verify before writing the caveat down' matters even for throwaway documentation — the first draft of the SKILL.md gap note claimed both sample_size and response_rate_pct were unmatched for Heimildin's construction, but direct testing showed response_rate_pct actually does resolve. Caught and corrected within this same round because the habit was to test the claim, not just describe the intended behavior.
highest_value_next_steps: A systematic chart-vs-prose completeness check (carried over from round 3, still untouched) — confirm chart extraction isn't silently missing a party the article's own prose discusses, on a sample large enough to trust rather than one-off spot checks.

Cross-source methodology comparison (round 4's Q25, newly identified as unable) — once two articles covering the same poll both have methodology fields populated, verify sample_size/response_rate_pct actually agree between RÚV and Vísir coverage of the same underlying poll.

VB discovery remains completely unstarted across all four rounds and is the largest single scope gap in the skill, though deliberately deprioritized behind correctness work on the already-built sources.
